data.title: Antofagasta Non-GAAP EPS of $1.29, revenue of $8.62B beats by $80M; reaffirms FY26 copper production guidance
data.description:
• Antofagasta press release (ANFGF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $1.29.
  • Revenue of $8.62B (+30.4% Y/Y) beats by $80M.
  • EBITDA was $5.2 billion, 52% higher on stronger revenues and robust cost control, which helped to increase the Group’s EBITDA margin to 60.3%.
  • Cash flow from operations increased by 30% to $4.3 billion.
  • Capital expenditure peaked in 2025 at $3.7 billion (2024: $2.4 billion), with major capital projects continuing in line with expectations.
  • Recommended final dividend of 48.0 cents per share. If approved, this would take full year distributions to the equivalent of a pay-out ratio of 50% of underlying net earnings per share, in line with the Company’s dividend policy.
  • The Group’s copper production guidance for 2026 remains unchanged at 650,000-700,000 tonnes. Cash costs before by-product credits and net cash costs are expected to be between $2.30/lb and $2.50/lb and between $1.15/lb and $1.35/lb, respectively.
  • In 2026, consolidated Group capital expenditure, which excludes Zaldívar, is expected to be $3.4 billion.

data.title: European indexes muted as AI concerns impact broader setiment
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.35% to 10,510. UK's unemployment rises more than estimated in Q4. The British pound fell below $1.36, touching its weakest level since February 5.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.01% to 24,795. Germany's inflation rate rises 2.1% Y/Y in January.

France (CAC:IND) +0.21% to 8,334. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.10% higher to 619.1, despite a drop seen in risk appetite with geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and AI concerns ruling market sentiment. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 3 basis points to 4.03%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 2.74%.

The UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.37%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: InterContinental Hotels Non-GAAP EPS of $5.01 misses by $0.01, adj. revenue of $2.47B beats by $10M
data.description:
• InterContinental Hotels press release (IHG): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $5.01 misses by $0.01.
  • Adjusted revenue of $2.47B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Global RevPAR1 +1.5%, with Americas +0.3%, EMEAA +4.6% and Greater China -1.6%.
  • Average daily rate +0.8%, occupancy +0.5%pts.
  • Total gross revenue $35.2bn, +5%.
  • Net cash from operating activities of $898m (2024: $724m) and adjusted free cash flow1 of $893m (2024: $655m).
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 of $1,332m, +12% YOY; net debt:adjusted EBITDA ratio of 2.5x.
  • Final dividend of 125.9¢ proposed, +10%, resulting in a total dividend for the year of 184.5¢, +10%.
  • New $950m buyback programme launched, which together with ordinary dividend payments is expected to return $1.2bn+ to shareholders in 2026, resulting in cumulative returns of more than $5bn over 5 years.

data.title: Elbit Systems wins contracts valued at ~$435M for advanced defense systems
data.description:
• Elbit Systems (ESLT) Tuesday said that it was awarded several contracts with a total value of approximately $435 million from an international customer.
  • Under these contracts, the company will supply a range of advanced systems, including land systems, and will also carry out a development program for an innovative defense solution.
  • The contracts will be performed over a period of six years.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: HIVE reports record $93.1M revenue; "dual-engine" strategy hits 25 EH/s hashrate
UK's unemployment rises more than estimated in Q4
data.description:
• The UK unemployment rate rose to 5.2% in the three months to December 2025, slightly above market expectations, which was forecast to remain unchanged at 5.1% from the previous period.
  • This marks its highest level since the three months to February 2021.

data.title: VAALCO Energy declares $0.0625 quarterly dividend
data.description:
• VAALCO Energy (EGY) declares $0.0625/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 5.07%
  • Payable March 27; for shareholders of record Feb. 27; ex-div Feb. 27.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.0625 for thirteen consecutive quarters.
  • See EGY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Elliot builts over 10% stake in Norwegian Cruise Line to push changes - report
data.description:
• Activist investor Elliott Investment Management has bought over a 10% stake in Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), positioning it as one of the company's largest shareholders, and intends to advocate for major operational and strategic changes to address underperformance, WSJ reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.
  • The cruise company has lagged competitors, with its shares down nearly 4% YTD, compared to gains of 4.5% for Carnival (CCL) and 15% for Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Elliott believes Norwegian (NCLH) could make changes to catch up to its rivals, the report added.
  • Elliot's focus is on improving the company's financial performance and the guest experience. Weak consumer spending on premium voyages, higher ship maintenance expenses, macroeconomic uncertainty, and fluctuating fuel prices are a few factors impacting the operator. 
  • Its shares sank on weak Q3 results and forecasts amid worrying cruise industry trends.
  • To note, the company recently appointed John W. Chidsey, former CEO of Subway and Burger King, as president and CEO effective February 12, 2026, replacing Harry Sommer. Chidsey, a board member since 2025, brings experience in consumer brand turnarounds.

data.title: HIVE Digital Technologies reports Q3 results
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies press release (HIVE): Q3 GAAP net loss of $91.3 million.
  • Revenue of $93.11M (+218.5% Y/Y) misses by $2.69M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA of $5.7 million.

  • Gross operating margin expanded significantly to $32.1 million (34.5%), up more than sixfold compared to $5.3 million in the prior year period.

  • Digital Currency Hashrate Revenue: $88.2 million, up 8% from Q2 FY2026, reflecting a 41% quarter-over-quarter increase in average hashrate to 22.9 EH/s, partially offset by approximately 10% lower Bitcoin prices and 15% higher network difficulty.

  • This hashrate revenue was achieved at a direct cost of $57.8 million, of which approximately 90% is energy costs. See the calculation of direct costs included below in this press release.

  • Bitcoin Output: Generated 885 Bitcoin, representing a 23% quarter over quarter increase, despite a 15% rise in network difficulty.

  • HPC Revenue: BUZZ HPC revenue was $4.9 million during the quarter. This revenue was achieved against direct costs of $2.3 million.

data.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy says it can weather Bitcoin drop to $8,000 while covering debt
data.description:
• Bitcoin-focused firm Strategy (MSTR) said that it has enough assets to withstand a drop in Bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) price to $8,000 while still fully covering its debt obligations.
  • "Strategy can withstand a drawdown in $BTC price to $8K and still have sufficient assets to fully cover our debt," the Michael Saylor-led company said on X.
  • The company, which holds more bitcoin than any other publicly traded company, has accumulated 714,644 BTC, worth roughly $49.3 billion at current prices, since adopting it as a treasury asset in 2020.
  • Strategy (MSTR) shares have, however, fallen nearly 60% over the past year.
